opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I had this sudden, ripping pain on my right side down low, and I felt so dizzy I almost passed out. (Her partner is on the call too and sounds really worried.)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The pain came on out of nowhere about two hours ago and it's excruciating, and she nearly fainted trying to get to the bathroom.

symptom_narrative.onset: sudden — started about 2 hours ago, came on all at once
symptom_narrative.duration: about 2 hours and constant since it started
symptom_narrative.character: sharp, like a ripping or tearing feeling
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst pain she's ever had — she can barely move and is lying dead still
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: low down on the right side of her belly and around the pubic area
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant, not coming in waves, and not easing off
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing helps — she took paracetamol and it did nothing
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: trying to sit up or stand makes the dizziness much worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: she feels really lightheaded and nearly blacked out, and she looks pale and clammy
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: can't stand up without almost fainting; lying completely still on the bed, very frightened

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: paracetamol (took some earlier for the pain)

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: at home with her partner, who is present and very distressed

objective_data_offered.value: her partner says she looks really pale and is sweating a lot
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: partner's visual impression over the phone, not a measured finding

objective_data_offered.value: she's lying completely still and can't sit up or stand without nearly passing out
a small amount of dark, brownish spotting since yesterday — much lighter than a normal period
